title: Federal Reserve Board - 2020 FOMC Press Releases
summary: This page is an index aggregating links to Federal Reserve FOMC statements and economic projections released throughout 2020, a period that included unprecedented emergency rate cuts to near zero in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. The page does not contain the full text of any individual FOMC statement; it only lists dates and titles of 13 separate FOMC communications from throughout the year.
key_points: The page is a listing/index aggregating 13 separate FOMC press releases from calendar year 2020
Listed items include FOMC statements from January, March (4 statements), April, June, July, September, and November 2020
Economic projections were released alongside statements in June, September, and December 2020
The actual content of each FOMC decision must be retrieved from individual linked pages
The source contains no full policy text, vote tallies, or forward guidance language
Page last updated February 26, 2026, but content relates to 2020 events
why_it_matters: This listing page provides no actionable policy content for RAG or research purposes. Individual FOMC statements from 2020 (including the two emergency cuts in March) are historically significant but must be retrieved from their respective URLs. The 2020 FOMC meetings included the Fed's response to COVID-19, cutting rates to 0-0.25% and launching emergency facilities.
macro_context: This is a navigation aid, not a macro catalyst source. The 2020 FOMC actions it references included the March 3 and March 15 emergency rate cuts to 0-0.25%, emergency lending facilities, and balance sheet expansion—key pandemic-era policy responses.

notes: NON-ARCHIVEABLE AS STANDALONE SOURCE: This page is a listing/index that aggregates links to 2020 FOMC press releases. It contains no full policy text. Individual FOMC statements from 2020 should be retrieved and archived separately. The relevant historical decisions include: March 3, 2020 emergency cut (50 bps), March 15, 2020 emergency cut to 0-0.25%, and subsequent meetings maintaining that range. This is historical material from 2020, over 5 years old. Low research utility in current form.
